conversation.text: Every day, fetch the top story from Hacker News and post its title and URL to Slack #news (C04NEWS0001).
Actually, I changed my mind — post to #engineering (C04ENGINEER1) instead of #news, and make it the top 3 stories, not just one.

executionScenarios.name: happy-path
executionScenarios.description: API returns several stories, the top 3 are posted to the corrected channel
executionScenarios.dataSetup: The schedule trigger fires. The Hacker News HTTP request returns a list of at least 5 stories, each with a title and url — e.g. 'Show HN: A new Rust web framework', 'Why we migrated off Kubernetes', 'The state of WebAssembly in 2026', 'Postgres 18 is out', 'Ask HN: best note-taking setup?'. The Slack post-message node returns a success response.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The workflow executes without errors. The stories are posted to #engineering (C04ENGINEER1) — the corrected channel — and NOT to #news (C04NEWS0001). Exactly the top 3 stories are included (the corrected count), not a single story. Each posted entry includes a title and a URL.

executionScenarios.name: fewer-than-three-stories
executionScenarios.description: The API returns fewer than 3 stories
executionScenarios.dataSetup: The schedule trigger fires. The Hacker News HTTP request returns only 2 stories, each with a title and url. The Slack post-message node returns a success response.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The workflow handles having fewer than 3 stories gracefully — it posts the 2 available stories to #engineering (C04ENGINEER1) without erroring on the missing third.
